papergraph.abstract: Developing cost-effective electrocatalysts for overall water splitting is an effective approach to produce green hydrogen and oxygen. NiFeSe4/NiSe2 heterostructure catalyst for high-efficiency HER and OER was effectively created using an...

keynotes.title: High-efficiency NiFeSe4/NiSe2 bifunctional electrocatalyst with outstanding oxygen evolution reaction and overall water splitting performance

keynote.title: High-efficiency NiFeSe4/NiSe2 bifunctional electrocatalyst with outstanding oxygen evolution reaction and overall water splitting performance
keynote.core_problem: Developing cost-effective electrocatalysts for overall water splitting to produce green hydrogen and oxygen.
contributions: Development of a NiFeSe4/NiSe2 heterostructure catalyst for high-efficiency hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) and oxygen evolution reaction (OER).

keynotes.title: Ir4+-Doped NiFe LDH to expedite hydrogen evolution kinetics as a Pt-like electrocatalyst for water splitting.

keynote.title: Ir4+-Doped NiFe LDH to expedite hydrogen evolution kinetics as a Pt-like electrocatalyst for water splitting
keynote.core_problem: Slow hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) kinetics in alkaline water splitting, requiring high-performance, non-precious electrocatalysts to replace Pt.
contributions: Development of Ir4+-doped NiFe layered double hydroxide (LDH) as a highly active HER electrocatalyst
Demonstration of Pt-like HER activity in alkaline media
methods: Hydrothermal synthesis of Ir4+-doped NiFe LDH
Electrochemical characterization (LSV, Tafel, EIS, stability testing)
Structural and compositional analysis (XRD, XPS, TEM, HAADF-STEM)

keynotes.title: A Mn-doped Ni2P nanosheet array: an efficient and durable hydrogen evolution reaction electrocatalyst in alkaline media.

keynote.title: A Mn-doped Ni2P nanosheet array: an efficient and durable hydrogen evolution reaction electrocatalyst in alkaline media
keynote.core_problem: Developing efficient, durable, and cost-effective electrocatalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) in alkaline media.
